Potential repricing windows

Macro calendar

Macro calendar stays dense across inflation, Fed guidance, labor, and bond-market tone.

Disinflation is improving, but yields and policy communication still cap full aggression.

Macro conditions still allow selective risk-taking, yet yields rising and policy uncertainty elevated keep the market from becoming a clean all-clear.

Signal

inflation cooling

Signal

yields rising

Signal

growth weakening

Signal

policy uncertainty elevated

US CPI

Critical

United States | 2026-03-18 | Inflation

Consensus: 2.9% YoY

Shelter and services disinflation breadth

This release will decide whether the market can keep leaning into disinflation without repricing Fed timing.

FOMC Rate Decision

Critical

United States | 2026-03-20 | Central Bank

Consensus: Hold

Dot-plot median and chair tone on inflation persistence

The meeting sets the policy narrative for the next several weeks across rates, USD, and leadership breadth.

US Initial Claims and payroll revisions

High

United States | 2026-03-21 | Labor

Consensus: Claims steady near cycle lows

Breadth of softening across hiring and wage revisions

Labor remains the main line between a soft landing and a sharper growth scare.

China Credit Impulse

High

China | 2026-03-22 | Liquidity

Consensus: Incremental easing

Magnitude of new credit versus targeted easing promises

This release matters for the durability of the global cyclical rebound and localization capex themes.

Eurozone flash PMIs

Moderate

Europe | 2026-03-24 | Growth

Consensus: Modest improvement

New orders versus services resilience

The data will show whether growth weakening is broadening beyond isolated US-sensitive pockets.

US 10Y auction and term-premium check

Moderate

United States | 2026-03-25 | Bonds

Consensus: Stable demand

Indirect bid participation and tail size

Bond-market tone is an underappreciated swing factor for factor leadership this month.

Geopolitical board

Shipping, conflict, and policy shocks keep the geopolitical overlay firmly active.

Overlay posture

Geopolitical risk is elevated but still tradable with selectivity.

Currencies
Risk sentiment
Inflation

Border conflict flare-up drives defense and haven demand

war/conflict

Critical

Escalating

Pushes safe-haven demand higher and caps aggression in Europe-linked cyclicals until the conflict premium fades.

conflict risk
safe havens
Europe exposure
reduce risk

Red Sea shipping corridor remains under intermittent threat

shipping/logistics disruption

High

Active

Keeps freight and energy risk premia elevated; favors resilient quality, select logistics names, and hedge exposure.

freight risk
energy premium
supply chain stress
add hedge

US-China semiconductor restrictions broaden

sanctions

High

Escalation risk

Creates headline volatility across chips but supports supply-chain localization and domestic capex beneficiaries.

export controls

headline volatility

Tariff rhetoric expands in EV and industrial supply chains

tariffs/trade war

High

Escalating

Supports localization winners but adds friction to EV, autos, and globally stretched manufacturing chains.

trade war
margin pressure
localization
avoid sector

OPEC+ discipline wobble keeps energy shock risk alive

energy shock

Moderate

Monitoring

Affects oil beta and inflation expectations directly, which can quickly tilt the regime away from duration-sensitive leadership.

oil beta
inflation risk
policy repricing
avoid sector

Election volatility rises across major democracies

elections/political instability

Moderate

Building

Policy uncertainty stays elevated and argues for cleaner setups, modest hedges, and more patience on crowded longs.

policy uncertainty
fiscal drift
headline volatility
monitor

Critical infrastructure cyber probe hits payment and shipping networks

cyber/regulatory shock

Moderate

Contained but live

Adds a regulatory and resilience premium to software/security names while exposing fragile operators to headline drawdowns.

cyber resilience
regulatory follow-up
operational risk
